mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike months are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in >60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of recovery-phase observations where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, a simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate indicates policy-induced economic cooling, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in >55% of months where both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which mechanically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where PAYEMS increases by >100k.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy compresses real corporate margins, forcing equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 195.0 threshold.
